website.title: Personal Website
website.description: The very website you're looking at! This project was created as a portfolio to showcase my experience and skills. I wanted to keep things simple for this site. Hope you like it!

grcnn.title: Green River College Nursing Nucleus
grcnn.description: I partnered up with a few classmates during my junior year of college to make a tool that centralizes resources and documents for students in Green River's Nursing program. We completed it over the span of a quarter (or about 5 sprints), and learned a lot about making something for a non-technical audience and working as part of a team. The site got handed over to senior students to complete as their capstone project once our time working on it was done.

library_project.title: Holman Library Data Dashboard
library_project.description: The capstone project for my Associate's Program, this project involved meeting with one of Green River's librarians and putting together a centralized source of data that pulled from an existing Google Site to visualize metrics about the substitute teaching program Green River's librarians serve as part of. I served as part of a team of people to clean data from an old Excel spreadsheet and hook this thing up. It's a lot more data-oriented compared to my software projects, and that's actually because my Associate's was in Data Analytics & Software Development, not pure software. We got the news a few months later that the librarian had actually integrated a lightly-modified version of this project into the librarians' toolset!

hoverworks.title: Hoverworks Ltd.
hoverworks.description: This was a project I built for my Cloud Application Deployment class during my time at Green River; it's meant to mock a storefront where users could purchase hoverboards (less like the ones with wheels that exist in the real world, more like "skateboard-shaped board that shoots pressurized air out the bottom to actually hover". It was created as a SPA that enables CRUD operations with a containerized MySQL database, and was made with React.js on the front-end, Express.js on the back-end, could be deployed as a Docker image, and was hosted on an IaaS instance via Google Cloud Platform's Compute Engine. A few years after graduating, I decided to go back and whip up some models in Blender to give it a fresh coat of paint. Click the link below to check it out!

photo_site.title: kassandraeller.com
photo_site.description: I was speaking with my sister once upon a time, and somehow the topic came to money and expenses. I learned that she'd been paying over $200 per year for a site that was 4 years out of date and unfinished (her words, not mine)! I decided to strike a deal with her: I'd go stay in the spare room of her townhouse for some time, and in exchange she'd get a new website that's a better value for her money. I took a few days off my weekend job to give myself plenty of time to complete this, and after about a week of work, the site was done. It felt good to save her some money; when the project was all wrapped up, Kasasndra told me the new site costs her about $XYZ per year.
